title: 10 best herbal creams for eye pain

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with eye pain because of its unique blend of soothing ingredients. The cream's active compounds, such as salicylic acid and beta-carotene, work together to reduce inflammation and ease tension in the delicate tissues around the eyes. As a result, it provides instant relief from eye strain, dryness, and discomfort caused by prolonged screen time, lack of sleep, or environmental factors. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also help to calm redness and puffiness, leaving your eyes feeling refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to eye pain.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: The herb contains flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with eye pain.

Vasodilatory effects: Ginkgo biloba's extracts can dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing pressure on the eyes, which may help alleviate tension headaches and eye strain-related pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Barks: The barks contain phenolic compounds that have antioxidant and analgesic effects, making it effective in reducing inflammation and pain associated with eye conditions.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 20 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and grind into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground ginkgo biloba powder with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Add 10 drops of vitamin e oil and stir well to combine; let it infuse for 24 hours at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another glass container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids.
Store the final cream in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to six months or until fully absorbed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi creams helps with eye pain because of its unique ability to stimulate blood flow to the eyes, reducing inflammation and soothing tired ocular tissues. The herbaceous extract in brahmi cream contains compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate redness, itchiness, and burning sensations associated with eye strain. Additionally, Brahmi's antioxidant properties protect the eyes from environmental stressors and oxidative damage, promoting overall eye health and comfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-inflammatory Properties: Brahmi creams contain antioxidants like Ashwagandha, which helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, thereby alleviating pain and discomfort.

Nerve Calming and Sedative Effects: The Brahmi plant contains compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce eye strain, fatigue, and irritation, leading to relief from pain and discomfort.

Tissue Regenerating and Wound Healing Properties: Brahmi creams contain compounds like Centella Asiatica, which stimulates collagen synthesis and promotes tissue repair, helping to accelerate wound healing and reducing the appearance of fine lines and dark circles around the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that help to soothe and calm eye irritation, reducing redness and inflammation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that effectively reduce swelling and puffiness around the eyes, providing relief from eye strain.
remedy_recipe: Grind 20g of dried bacopa monnieri leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix the powdered bacopa monnieri with 10g of beeswax and 5g of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 2g of vitamin e oil and 1g of aloe vera gel to the mixture and stir until well combined for 30 seconds.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 65°c before pouring it into a sterilized container for 5 minutes.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify at room temperature or in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with eye pain because they contain a unique combination of anti-inflammatory and soothing compounds that directly target the eyes. The gel-like texture of aloe vera provides a protective barrier, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by dryness or strain. Additionally, the antioxidants present in aloe creams help to calm and relax the muscles surrounding the eyes, providing fast relief from pain and tension.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from eye pain caused by inflammation.
Antioxidant property: Antioxidants present in aloe vera neutralize free radicals and protect the eyes from oxidative stress, which can contribute to eye pain and discomfort.
Soothing and moisturizing property: Aloe vera's soothing and moisturizing properties help calm dry, irritated, or itchy eyes, reducing pain and discomfort caused by dryness or irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in aloin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that soothes and calms irritated eyes.
Rhyzomes: Contain gel-like substances that help reduce inflammation and swelling around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera gel from mature plants extracting about 100ml.
Combine the aloe vera gel with 20g of cooled olive oil and 10g of beeswax in a heat-proof bowl.
Place the mixture over low heat allowing it to melt and mix thoroughly for 15 minutes.
Add 5 drops of peppermint essential oil and 5 drops of lavender essential oil to the melted mixture stirring well.
Pour the cooled and mixed cream into sterilized glass jars storing them in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with eye pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream, derived from the Echinacea flower, contains compounds that reduce swelling and discomfort in the eyes, providing quick relief from pain and redness. Additionally, the cream's soothing and moisturizing effects help to calm irritated eyes, making it an effective remedy for relieving eye strain, dryness, and discomfort caused by prolonged screen time or environmental factors.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as isobutylamides and triterpenoids, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, which can alleviate discomfort and pain.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of Kansas coneflower cream help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to eye pain.

Topical analgesic effects: The topical application of Kansas coneflower cream contains analgesic compounds that directly target the affected area, providing local relief from pain, itching, and discomfort in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, which are effective in reducing inflammation and relieving eye pain.
Flowers: Containing high levels of flavonoids, particularly kaempferol and quercetin, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties to alleviate eye discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia root powder and mix it with 20 grams of coconut oil in a heatproof glass container.
Combine the mixture with 100 milliliters of distilled water and stir until it forms a smooth paste, taking about 5 minutes to prepare.
Transfer the paste to a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over boiling water and simmer for 15-20 minutes to infuse the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil, taking about 10 minutes to complete.
Allow the infused oil to cool and thicken at room temperature for several hours before applying it as an eye cream, using 2-3 drops per application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with eye pain because its soothing properties can calm irritated eyes and reduce inflammation. The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, work to alleviate redness and discomfort caused by dry eyes, allergies, or exposure to digital screens for extended periods. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing effects help to hydrate the eyes, reducing irritation and itchiness that can exacerbate eye pain. As a result, English lavender creams provide natural relief from eye strain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to pain and discomfort.
Anxiolytic and sedative properties: The calming effects of lavender can help alleviate anxiety and promote relaxation, reducing the physical tension that can exacerbate eye pain.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, which can contribute to discomfort and pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lavender roots are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes.

Buds: Fresh lavender buds contain high amounts of volatile oils, including linalool and linalyl acetate, which have natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that can provide relief from eye pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried lavender flowers lavandula angustifolia 10% of the total weight of the cream and carrier oils in advance.
Combine 20g of beeswax pellets with 40ml of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat until melted slowly stirring occasionally for 30 minutes.
Add 4g of dried lavender flowers to the melted wax mixture and infuse for 10 minutes stirring occasionally allowing flavors to meld.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean mixing bowl discarding solids and discard any excess material retaining only clear liquid.
Combine the infused liquid with 40ml of sweet almond oil and 20g of shea butter in a mixing bowl whipping until smooth creamy texture achieved for 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with eye pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb's flavonoids, lutein, and zeaxanthin work together to soothe and calm irritated eyes. Marigold extract reduces redness and swelling, while its antioxidant properties neutralize free radicals that can cause eye strain and fatigue. Additionally, the cream's gentle, non-irritating formula makes it suitable for sensitive skin around the eyes, providing long-lasting relief from discomfort and promoting healthy vision.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Pot marigold cream has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from eye pain caused by inflammation or irritation.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in pot marigold cream help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the eyes, reducing the risk of eye pain and discomfort.

Softenening and lubricating properties: The emollients and oils in pot marigold cream help soften and lubricate the eyes, providing long-lasting relief from dryness, irritation, and itchiness that can contribute to eye pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by eye pain.
Flowers: The flowers contain antioxidants that soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and inflammation associated with eye pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality calendula officinalis flowers and dry them according to the recommended moisture content for infusion.
Measure out 50 grams of dried calendula flowers and combine with 500 milliliters of boiling water to create a strong tea infusion.
Steep the mixture for 20-30 minutes before straining it through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Combine the cooled tea infusion with 150 grams of melted coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler to create a creamy emulsion.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for at least 30 minutes before pouring it into sterilized jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail creams helps with eye pain because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Horsetail, a natural herb, contains compounds that have been shown to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the eyes. When applied topically as an eye cream, these compounds can penetrate the skin and reach the affected areas, providing relief from dryness, itchiness, and redness. Additionally, horsetail's antioxidant properties help protect the delicate tissues around the eyes from further damage, promoting overall eye health and comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail cream contains compounds like flavonoids and polyphenols, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, alleviating eye pain caused by conditions like conjunctivitis or blepharitis.

Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in horsetail cream, such as vitamin E and beta-carotene, help neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes' delicate tissues, reducing oxidative stress and associated pain.

Vasoconstrictive: Horsetail's vasoconstrictive properties, attributed to its flavonoid content, help narrow blood vessels in the eye, which can alleviate redness, itching, and burning sensations caused by conditions like conjunctivitis or irritated eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the field horsetail plant are used to create creams that help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Field horsetail rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease eye strain, making them a popular choice for eye creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh equisetum arvense plants ensuring only the green shoots are used.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 50°c for 24 hours.
Grind 20g of dried equisetum arvense into a fine powder using an electric spice grinder and sift through a fine-mesh sieve.
Combine the powdered equisetum arvense with 100ml of melted coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat, stirring occasionally for 30 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature before transferring to sterile glass jars for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with eye pain because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes. The cream's soothing properties calm dry, itchy, or irritated eyes, while its antioxidants combat oxidative stress caused by prolonged screen use or other environmental factors. Additionally, dandelion's natural astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels, reducing puffiness and inflammation around the eyes, providing instant relief from discomfort and fatigue.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion contains anti-inflammatory compounds like sesquiterpenes, which can help reduce swelling and redness associated with eye pain, making it easier to manage conditions like conjunctivitis or blepharitis.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion cream, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye pain and discomfort.

Soften and calm skin: Dandelion's emollient and soothing properties can help soften and calm irritated or inflamed skin around the eyes, reducing itchiness, burning, and stinging sensations associated with conditions like dry eyes, pink eye, or contact lens irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing eye inflammation and promoting healing.
Seeds: Containing fatty acids and vitamins A and E, they help to soothe and moisturize the eyes, providing relief from dryness and itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh or dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers.
Combine the herbs with 50 milliliters of carrier oil such as olive or coconut oil.
Infuse the mixture in a dark glass bottle for 14 days, shaking daily for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Add 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil, heating until melted and smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chickweed creams helps with eye pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and itching in the eyes. The cream's soothing and calming effects also ease discomfort and irritation caused by dryness, allergies, or minor injuries. Additionally, chickweed's antioxidant compounds help to protect the eyes from oxidative stress, promoting overall eye health and reducing the risk of chronic pain. By applying this natural remedy topically, individuals can experience fast and effective relief from eye pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chickweed contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoid saponins, which help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the eyes.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in chickweed cream, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye inflammation and damage, promoting healthy vision and reducing discomfort.

Soothing and calming agents: Chickweed's mucilages (gelling compounds) have a soothing and calming effect on the eyes, providing relief from itchiness, redness, and dryness associated with eye pain and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Buds: Containing high levels of flavonoids, which can reduce inflammation and promote blood flow to the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of fresh stellaria media flowers on a sunny day in summer.
Dry the harvested flowers at room temperature for 7-10 days or using a drying rack for 3-4 hours.
Grind 100 grams of dried stellaria media flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the ground powder with 50 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler until melted and well combined.
Remove from heat, let cool to around 60 degrees celsius before pouring into sterilized containers and refrigerating for at least 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort creams helps with eye pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The herbal extract contains hyperforin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from conditions such as conjunctivitis and blepharitis. Additionally, St John's wort cream can help soothe irritated eyes and reduce pressure and tension around the orbital bone, making it a popular natural remedy for eye pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's Wort has been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling, which can alleviate eye pain caused by conditions such as conjunctivitis or blepharitis.
Antioxidant: The cream's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye strain and discomfort.
Analgesic: St. John's Wort contains compounds that have a mild analgesic effect, which can help numb or reduce pain in the eyes due to conditions such as dry eye syndrome or episcleritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain a high concentration of hyperforin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate eye pain.
Barks: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe eye irritation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort (hypericum perforatum) flowers at dawn on a dry sunny day ensuring maximum potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm oven at 40°c for 12 hours or by air drying for 7 days.
Combine 30g of dried st john's wort with 150ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 6 hours ensuring the oil has extracted the medicinal properties of the herb.
Strain and filter the final product through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a sterilized glass jar for storage and use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with eye pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb fennel contains a compound called anethole, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the body. When applied topically as a cream, it can help alleviate dryness, itchiness, and redness associated with eye irritation. Additionally, the cream's soothing properties can calm irritated nerves, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with eye injuries or conditions like conjunctivitis.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant capacity of fennel cream helps protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye pain and discomfort.

Vasodilatory properties: Fennel cream's ability to dilate blood vessels helps improve blood flow to the eyes, which can alleviate tension and pressure that may be contributing to eye pain or headaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, fennel roots can be used to create soothing topical creams that alleviate eye irritation and reduce puffiness.
Buds: Fennel buds contain a unique combination of flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to calm inflammation and reduce redness in the eyes, making them an effective ingredient for eye pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare leaves from a trusted source in late summer or early fall.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Crush 100 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the crushed herb with 200 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a glass jar and let it steep for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the infused oil from the solids then store the cream in an amber-colored glass jar.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry creams helps with eye pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, work together to reduce swelling and ease irritation in the eyes. This natural relief can provide instant comfort for individuals suffering from conditions such as conjunctivitis, dry eye syndrome, or even minor scratches on the cornea. Additionally, highbush cranberry creams have been shown to possess antioxidant properties, which can help protect the delicate tissues of the eye and promote overall ocular health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Highbush cranberry cream contains antioxidants like vitamin C and flavonoids, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, alleviating discomfort and pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as salicylic acid and quercetin, can reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from eye pain caused by conditions like conjunctivitis or dry eye syndrome.

Vasodilatory properties: Highbush cranberry contains vasodilators that can help dilate blood vessels in the eyes, improving blood flow and reducing pressure on the optic nerve, which may contribute to eye pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the highbush cranberry plant have been traditionally used to treat eye inflammation and redness, making them an ideal ingredient in creams for eye pain.
Fruits: The fruits of the plant contain anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of vaccinium macrocarpon leaves, clean them thoroughly and dry them in a shaded area for 24 hours.
Combine 200g of dried vaccinium macrocarpon leaves with 400ml of water and simmer the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth and discard the solids; then add 20% glycerin to the extracted juice and mix well.
Combine 100g of beeswax, 50g of coconut oil, and 25g of shea butter in a double boiler, heating them until melted and smooth.
Gradually add the vaccinium macrocarpon extract to the melted wax mixture while stirring continuously, allowing it to cool and thicken for 10 minutes before filling into containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with eye pain because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and relieve tension in the eyes. The anti-inflammatory properties of yarrow extract work to soothe irritated eyes, while its analgesic effects help to numb any discomfort or pain. Additionally, the cream's antioxidant properties help to protect the delicate tissues around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall eye health and reducing the risk of long-term eye problems. As a result, yarrow creams provide natural relief for those suffering from eye pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow cream contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the eyes caused by conditions such as conjunctivitis or blepharitis.
Antimicrobial: Yarrow's antimicrobial compounds, including volatile oils and terpenes, can help prevent bacterial infections that can cause eye pain, redness, and discharge, such as pink eye.

Wound-healing and anti-scarring: The saponins in yarrow cream promote wound healing and reduce the formation of scar tissue, which can be beneficial for eye conditions such as dry eye or blepharospasm that require prolonged healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving eye pain and reducing inflammation.
Flowers: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids and terpenes, which provide antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects to soothe and calm irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers from your local botanical or online supplier.
Combine the harvested herbs with 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 4 weeks at room temperature allowing the herbal extract to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Whip 50g of beeswax and 100ml of coconut oil together until smooth then blend with the infused herbal oil and let cool.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with eye pain because they contain a unique combination of natural ingredients that work together to soothe and calm irritated eyes. The active compounds in valerian root, such as valerenic acid, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and redness, while the moisturizing properties of emollients and oils hydrate the delicate skin around the eyes. As a result, herbal valerian creams can effectively relieve tension headaches, eye strain, and dryness associated with computer use, reading, or other visual activities.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anti-inflammatory effects: Valerian's sedative properties can help reduce eye inflammation and irritation, while its anti-inflammatory effects can alleviate redness and swelling.
Antispasmodic properties: Valerian's ability to relax smooth muscle tissue can help relieve spasms in the eyelids and surrounding muscles, which can contribute to eye pain and tension.

Antioxidant activity: Valerian's antioxidant properties can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to age-related macular degeneration and other eye problems that may cause pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in valerenic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and relax the eyes to alleviate pain.
Rhyzomes: Contain sesquiterpenes that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in soothing eye discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality valeriana officinalis root and dried it thoroughly for future use.
Grind 20 grams of dried valeriana root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered valeriana root with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mixture reaches a temperature of 60 degrees celsius.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into sterilized glass jars and let it cool before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with eye pain because of its unique combination of soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as elderberry extract and chamomile oil, work together to calm irritated eyes and reduce swelling. This relief can be especially beneficial for individuals who experience eye strain or dryness from prolonged screen time, reading, or other visual activities. By applying a thin layer of elder cream around the orbital bone, users can enjoy fast-acting and natural pain relief without relying on harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Elder cream's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to conditions like dry eye syndrome, conjunctivitis, and other forms of eye pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herbal extracts in elder cream, such as flavonoids and terpenes, possess anti-inflammatory activity that helps soothe and calm irritated or inflamed eyes, reducing pain and discomfort.

Emollient and soothing properties: Elder cream's moisturizing agents, including fatty acids and glycerin, help hydrate and nourish the delicate skin around the eyes, providing a protective barrier against irritants and soothing dryness and itchiness associated with eye pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain bioactive compounds like terpenes and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in soothing eye pain.
Rhyzomes: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, they help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, providing relief from eye discomfort and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried sambucus nigra flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground sambucus nigra flowers with 100 grams of beeswax and 200 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture reaches 70°c.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let it cool to room temperature while allowing the mixture to thicken into a smooth consistency.
Transfer the cooled mixture to an airtight container and store it in the refrigerator for up to 6 months, shaking well before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with eye pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The flavonoids present in licorice root, such as glabridin and isoliquiritigenin, have been shown to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the eyes. Additionally, licorice creams can help soothe and calm irritated eyes, providing fast relief from conditions like dry eye syndrome, conjunctivitis, and blepharitis. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, herbal licorice creams offer a natural solution for alleviating eye pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and glabridin, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort in the eyes.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal licorice creams contain antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which neutralize free radicals and protect the eyes from oxidative stress, reducing pain and promoting overall eye health.

Soften and moisten tissues: Licorice root has been shown to have a soothing effect on mucous membranes, including those in the eyes, helping to soften and moisten dry or irritated tissues, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that reduces inflammation and relieves eye strain.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Barks: Contains flavonoids that have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the eyes from damage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 15 minutes.
Combine the powdered root with 10 grams of beeswax, 5 grams of coconut oil, and 2.5 grams of olive oil in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture until it reaches a smooth consistency, adding 1 gram of vitamin e oil and 0.5 grams of aloe vera gel as needed.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to room temperature before whipping it into a creamy texture using an electric mixer for 5 minutes.
Fill sterilized tubes or containers with the cooled cream, storing them in the refrigerator at a consistent temperature below 40 degrees fahrenheit for up to two weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose creams helps with eye pain because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and discomfort in the eyes. The herbs used in these creams, such as calendula and chamomile, have been shown to soothe dry, irritated eyes and calm redness. Additionally, beach rose creams often contain soothing aloe vera and coconut oil that moisturize and protect the delicate skin around the eyes, providing long-lasting relief from eye strain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Beach rose creams containing anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids may help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, alleviating discomfort and pain.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in beach rose creams can neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress, which is believed to play a role in eye strain, dryness, and irritation, helping to soothe and protect the eyes.

Calming and soothing effects: Beach rose creams containing calming herbs like chamomile or lavender may help relax and calm the surrounding skin, reducing redness and itchiness associated with eye pain and promoting a sense of comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to reduce puffiness and soothe irritated eyes.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in treating eye pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa rugosa petals and dry them in a warm place for several days.
Combine 20 grams of dried rosa rugosa petals with 100 milliliters of organic olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a dark cabinet for 14 to 21 days, shaking the jar daily, to allow infusion.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids, then add 20 grams of beeswax pellets.
Melt the beeswax over low heat for 10 to 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until a smooth cream forms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with eye pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The cream contains citral, a compound that reduces swelling and relieves discomfort in the eyes. When applied topically to the affected area, lemongrass cream can help to soothe irritated eyes and alleviate pressure from headaches and tension. Additionally, the calming scent of lemongrass can promote relaxation, further reducing eye strain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Lemongrass contains compounds like citral and geraniol, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and ease eye pain caused by inflammation.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of lemongrass, particularly its high levels of flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help neutralize free radicals in the eyes, reducing oxidative stress and damage that may contribute to eye pain.

Anxiolytic property: The calming and relaxing effects of lemongrass essential oil, which is often used in herbal creams, can help reduce anxiety and stress-related eye tension, making it easier for people with eye pain to relax and alleviate their discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemongrass roots have been traditionally used to treat eye issues due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them an effective ingredient in soothing eye creams.

Buds: The buds of the lemongrass plant contain a high concentration of essential oils that help reduce eye inflammation and discomfort, making them a popular choice for incorporating into eye care products.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered cymbopogon citratus with 100ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler at 70°c for 30 minutes.
Add 20g of beeswax pellets to the mixture and continue heating until it reaches a temperature of 80°c.
Remove from heat and let it cool and thicken for 2 hours before whipping with an electric mixer for 5 minutes.
Transfer the cream to sterilized containers and store them in the refrigerator at a temperature below 8°c for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop creams helps with eye pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and redness around the eyes. The cream's active ingredients, such as passiflora incarnata, work to calm irritated tissues and ease discomfort. Additionally, maypop's natural analgesic properties help to numb the area, providing fast relief from stabbing or throbbing eye pain. As a result, herbal maypop creams offer a gentle and non-invasive solution for soothing eye strain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Passionflower (Passiflora incarnata): Passionflower contains flavonoids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and sedative properties, helping to reduce eye inflammation and promote relaxation, thus alleviating eye pain.

Vitamin K: Maypop creams often contain vitamin K, which has been found to improve blood circulation and reduce swelling in the eyes, thereby reducing eye pain caused by conditions such as conjunctivitis or orbital cellulitis.

Glycyrrhizin (Licorice root): Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from eye pain associated with conditions like dry eye syndrome or allergic reactions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids and flavonoids, Maypop roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Flowers: The delicate petals of the Maypop flowers contain gentle astringent compounds that can reduce redness and inflammation around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried passiflora incarnata flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Combine the powdered herbs with 200ml of sweet almond oil and 20ml of beeswax in a heat-resistant glass measuring cup for 10 minutes.
Heat the mixture over low heat on a double boiler or in a saucepan set in a larger pot filled with simmering water, stirring occasionally, for 30 minutes until the wax is fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 40°c, then add 10ml of vitamin e oil and stir well to combine for 5 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars or tubes, seal tightly, and store in a dark cupboard at room temperature for up to 6 months before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for eye pain are a natural and effective solution to alleviate discomfort, irritation, and inflammation caused by dry eyes, allergies, or injuries. These topical creams combine plant extracts with soothing ingredients to calm and moisturize the delicate skin around the eyes. Examples of herbal creams that help with eye pain include those containing aloe vera, chamomile, and green tea, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. By providing instant relief from itching, burning, and redness, these creams significantly improve daily lives by allowing individuals to perform tasks without discomfort and enjoy better sleep quality.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat eye pain with herbal creams is by applying topically cooled creams containing aloe vera, chamomile, or peppermint. These herbs have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort. For optimal results, apply the cream gently over the affected area, avoiding direct contact with the eyes. Repeat application as needed to alleviate eye pain and promote relaxation.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye pain that are treated with herbal creams are headaches, migraines, and facial tension. Herbal creams containing ingredients like peppermint, eucalyptus, and lavender can help soothe and relax the skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Other conditions such as earaches, sinus pressure, and even dental issues can also be alleviated with topical herbal remedies.
